pattern: The trick n & (n-1) always clears exactly the lowest set bit, so repeating it until n reaches zero counts set bits in O(popcount) time with no division or string conversion.
hints: The naive approach scans all 32 bit positions with a mask — correct but independent of how many bits are actually set. Can you skip directly from one set bit to the next?

Subtracting 1 from any non-zero integer flips all bits from the rightmost 1 down to bit 0; ANDing the original with that result therefore clears exactly that one set bit and leaves everything else unchanged.
Loop while n != 0, executing n = n & (n - 1) each iteration and incrementing a counter — the loop body runs exactly as many times as there are set bits.

edge_inputs.description: statement example 2: exactly one bit set
minimum value 1 has exactly one set bit
large power of two has only one set bit despite high magnitude
alternating bits 0101... to check no double-counting

stress.description: random integers across the full positive 31-bit range
